mechanism_narrative: Continuous declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.0% within a single month, labor market deterioration triggers risk-off positioning and lower earnings expectations, depressing S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a decline in real retail sales indicate synchronized demand weakness, forcing equity risk premiums higher and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint decline of INDPRO and RSAFS fails to predict negative 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of historical instances, or the interaction term coefficient in a multivariate regression is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: During periods where the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, aggressive tightening compresses corporate profit margins via higher financing costs, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or statistically neutral when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5% and CPIAUCSL rises concurrently, or the predictive power vanishes after controlling for VIX levels.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in housing starts reflects underlying consumer confidence and construction activity, mechanically supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon through sectoral earnings momentum.
failure_condition: HOUST growth consistently precedes negative 5-day SPX returns, or the rolling Spearman correlation between HOUST changes and forward returns remains below 0.05 over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index falls below 105.0, supply chain bottlenecks resolve but signal weakening aggregate demand, prompting a repricing of cyclical equities and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly lower following INDPRO breaches below 105.0, or the signal produces a positive Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising payroll employment and stagnant real retail sales indicates labor income is not translating into consumption, triggering a downward revision in corporate revenue forecasts and depressing S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The divergence between PAYEMS growth and RSAFS stagnation fails to predict negative 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of historical instances, or the interaction term coefficient in a multivariate regression is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion where quarterly GDP growth exceeds 3.0% and CPI inflation surpasses 3.0%, overheating forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity valuations expand despite simultaneous GDP and CPI breaches, or the forward return differential between overheating and non-overheating regimes is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).
